theory.intro: Unknown-angle problems with parallel lines often require a chain: transfer an angle using a parallel-line property, then use a relationship at one point.
facts: Corresponding and alternate angles on parallel lines are equal.
Co-interior angles on parallel lines add to $180^{\circ}$.
Angles on a straight line add to $180^{\circ}$; angles at a point add to $360^{\circ}$; vertically opposite angles are equal.
Write a reason at every step so the chain can be checked.

foundation.q: The horizontal lines are parallel. Find $x$ and give reasons.
The horizontal lines are parallel. A $47^{\circ}$ angle is vertically opposite angle $y$, and $y$ is alternate to $x$. Find $x$.
The horizontal lines are parallel. A co-interior angle with $y$ is $115^{\circ}$, and $x$ is vertically opposite $y$. Find $x$.
At the upper intersection, adjacent angles of $35^{\circ}$ and $55^{\circ}$ together correspond to $x$ at the lower intersection. Find $x$.
An angle of $128^{\circ}$ is alternate to $y$. Angle $x$ is adjacent to $y$ on a straight line. Find $x$.

development.q: Parallel lines are crossed by a transversal. A $62^{\circ}$ angle corresponds to $a$. Angle $x$ is co-interior with $a$. Find $x$.
Parallel lines are crossed by a transversal. An angle of $139^{\circ}$ is vertically opposite $a$. Angle $x$ is co-interior with $a$. Find $x$.
At the first parallel line, a straight angle is split into $72^{\circ}$ and $a$. Angle $a$ is alternate to $x$. Find $x$.

Two parallel lines are crossed by two transversals. One transversal transfers $36^{\circ}$ to angle $a$; the other transfers $74^{\circ}$ to adjacent angle $b$ at the same point. Find the remaining angle $x$ on the straight line.
An angle of $126^{\circ}$ corresponds to $a$. At the same lower intersection, $a$ and $x$ are vertically opposite after $a$ is first transferred across the parallel lines. Find $x$.

mastery.q: Two horizontal lines are parallel and $P$ lies between them. A ray from $P$ meets the upper line at $37^{\circ}$, and another ray from $P$ meets the lower line at $48^{\circ}$, as shown. By drawing an auxiliary line through $P$ parallel to the two lines, find the angle $x$ between the rays.

Two parallel lines are crossed by two rays meeting at point $P$ between them. After drawing an auxiliary parallel through $P$, the transferred angles on one side are $42^{\circ}$ and $67^{\circ}$. Find the reflex angle between the rays.

Three parallel lines are crossed by two transversals meeting on the middle parallel. A corresponding angle from the upper line is $58^{\circ}$ and an alternate angle from the lower line is $49^{\circ}$. Find the angle between the two transversals above the middle line.
